questions.question: What was the Permanent Settlement?
questions.correct_answer: A land revenue system introduced by the British in Bengal in 1793.
questions.explanation: Under this system, zamindars were made responsible for collecting land revenue.

questions.question: Who introduced the Permanent Settlement?
questions.explanation: He introduced it to ensure a fixed and regular revenue for the British government.

questions.question: Who were zamindars?
questions.correct_answer: Landowners responsible for collecting revenue from peasants.
questions.explanation: They had to pay a fixed revenue to the British government.

questions.question: What happened if zamindars failed to pay revenue?
questions.explanation: The British auctioned the land to recover revenue.

questions.question: What was indigo used for?
questions.explanation: Indigo dye was used in European textile industries.

questions.question: Why did British planters force peasants to grow indigo?
questions.correct_answer: To supply indigo dye to European markets.
questions.explanation: Indigo was in high demand in Europe.

questions.question: What was the Indigo Revolt?
questions.correct_answer: A protest by peasants against forced indigo cultivation.
questions.explanation: Peasants refused to grow indigo and protested against planters.

questions.question: When did the Indigo Revolt take place?
questions.explanation: It was a major peasant protest against indigo planters.

questions.question: Why did peasants dislike growing indigo?
questions.correct_answer: It reduced land for food crops and damaged the soil.
questions.explanation: Farmers could not grow enough food for survival.

questions.question: Who were moneylenders?
questions.correct_answer: People who provided loans to peasants.
questions.explanation: Peasants often borrowed money to pay taxes or debts.

notes.title: Permanent Settlement
notes.content: Introduced in 1793, this system fixed the land revenue to be paid by zamindars to the British government.

notes.title: Zamindars
notes.content: They were responsible for collecting land revenue from peasants and paying it to the British.

notes.title: Indigo Cultivation
notes.content: Peasants were forced to grow indigo by European planters to produce blue dye.

notes.title: Indigo Revolt
notes.content: In 1859–60, peasants protested against forced indigo cultivation.

notes.title: Rural Economy
notes.content: Heavy taxes and loans from moneylenders caused many peasants to fall into debt.

facts.fact: The Permanent Settlement was introduced in Bengal in 1793.
Indigo dye was exported to Europe for textile production.
The Indigo Revolt took place in Bengal during 1859–1860.
Many peasants fell into debt due to heavy land taxes.
